mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes against 20-day returns falls below 0.02, or positive returns do not significantly exceed baseline during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases by over 0.3pp, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth expectations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to generate a positive mean 20-day forward return, or the interaction term loses predictive power with p > 0.10 in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, a reversal to positive month-over-month growth signals supply-side stabilization, prompting institutional rebalancing into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO turns positive after 2 consecutive months of decline
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns following the specified INDPRO reversal are not significantly positive with t-stat < 1.65, or the mechanism underperforms during actual recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and wage income expansion, which supports consumer spending and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS changes against 20-day returns remains below 0.01, or positive payroll surprises consistently coincide with negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate inflation fears and expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing rapid equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold signal shows no predictive accuracy above random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and accelerating retail sales indicates a shift toward consumption-driven demand, which boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDP growth and retail sales changes fails to predict 60-day returns with p > 0.10, or the signal yields negative alpha during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening labor market, a simultaneous rise in unemployment and decline in payrolls confirms a structural labor shock, forcing equity risk premium repricing and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases while PAYEMS declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint labor shock signal fails to generate negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered cases, or the mechanism loses predictive power when labor markets are actually weakening.
